name: Quadrax
description: 4-channel CV-controllable function generator. Each channel independently operates as AD/AHR envelope, cycling LFO, pulse burst generator, or morphing bipolar LFO. Channels chainable via flexible link options.

controls.name: Rise
controls.description: Attack time (AD/AHR/CYCLE) or pulse rate (BURST) or LFO rate (LFO). CCW = fastest.

controls.name: Fall
controls.description: Decay/release time (AD/AHR/CYCLE), burst length (BURST), or LFO morph (LFO). CCW = fastest.

controls.name: Shape
controls.description: Response curve shape for Rise and Fall. Noon = linear. STANDARD/ALTERNATE alters behavior.
controls.detail: STANDARD: CCW = logarithmic · CW = exponential · ALTERNATE: inverts per stage

controls.name: LED
controls.description: Amplitude indicator. Green = positive voltage, red = negative voltage.

controls.name: LINK/CV Button
controls.description: Short press cycles link mode (No Link, Trigger, EOR, EOF). Long press enters CV Assignment mode. If own TRIG is also patched, link source + TRIG are OR'd (Multiple Triggers).
controls.detail: CH2 triggered by CH1 · CH3 by CH2 · CH4 by CH3 · CH1 by CH4

controls.name: MODE/DESTINATION Button
controls.description: Short press cycles mode: AD (unlit) → AHR (red) → CYCLE (green) → BURST (yellow) → LFO (magenta). Long press toggles STANDARD/ALTERNATE operation.
controls.detail: In CV assign mode: sets destination (Rise/Fall/Shape/Level) and attenuversion amount

inputs.name: TRIG 1–4
inputs.description: Trigger/gate input per channel. Fires envelope, resets CYCLE/LFO, clocks BURST. LFO uses as clock for beat-sync.

inputs.name: CV A–D
inputs.description: Four freely-assignable CV inputs. Each can modulate Rise, Fall, Shape, or Level on any channel via internal CV matrix with per-assignment attenuversion.

outputs.name: OUT 1–4
outputs.description: Main output per channel. AD/AHR/CYCLE/BURST: unipolar 0–5V or 0–10V (user selectable). LFO: bipolar ±5V.

behaviors.name: AD Mode
behaviors.description: Classic attack/decay envelope. Completes full cycle on trigger; ignores gate length. RISE = attack, FALL = decay, SHAPE = curve. ALTERNATE mode inverts curve assignment per stage.

behaviors.name: AHR Mode
behaviors.description: Attack/Hold/Release envelope. Attacks on gate rise, sustains at peak while gate is high, releases on gate fall. RISE = attack, FALL = release, SHAPE = curve.

behaviors.name: CYCLE Mode
behaviors.description: Cycling unipolar LFO. RISE + FALL set frequency and shape of cycle. Trigger resets phase. GATE input times ignored.

behaviors.name: BURST Mode
behaviors.description: Pulse burst generator. RISE sets pulse rate (0.05Hz–3.33kHz), FALL sets burst envelope length (0.3ms–20s), SHAPE defines individual pulse shape and overall amplitude curve. Alternate: tilting sawtooth shape.

behaviors.name: LFO Mode (Standard)
behaviors.description: Bipolar ±5V LFO. RISE = rate (free) or clock multiplier/divider (clocked, ±64×). SHAPE selects waveshape, FALL morphs the waveform. Multiple clocked LFOs can phase-sync.

behaviors.name: LFO Mode (Alternate / LFV)
behaviors.description: Chaotic Low Frequency Vacillator. RISE = vacillation rate, FALL = max variance per cycle, SHAPE = slew amount. Creates organic, non-repeating modulation.

behaviors.name: Channel Linking
behaviors.description: Three link modes: Trigger (simultaneous fire), EOR (fire at attack peak), EOF (fire at decay end). Enables multi-stage envelope chains. Combining link with own TRIG OR's the two sources.

patchIdeas.name: Four-stage ADSR chain
patchIdeas.patch: CH1 AD · CH2–4 AD · CH2 EOR-linked to CH1, CH3 EOF-linked to CH2, CH4 EOF-linked to CH3 · one trigger cascades through all four.
patchIdeas.diagram:
QUADRAX:
* CH 1: AD, EOR link
* CH 2-4: AD, EOF link
- Gate Source (OUT) t> QUADRAX (TRIG 1)
- QUADRAX (OUT 1) >> VCA 1 (CV)
- QUADRAX (OUT 2) >> VCA 2 (CV)
- QUADRAX (OUT 3) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- QUADRAX (OUT 4) >> VCA Final (CV)
steps: Patch a gate source into CH1 TRIG.
Short-press CH1 MODE until it is unlit (AD mode).
Short-press CH2 MODE until unlit, then short-press CH2 LINK until it turns green (EOR link to CH1).
Repeat for CH3 and CH4 but cycle LINK until yellow (EOF link).
Set CH1 RISE fast, FALL medium; CH2 RISE/FALL medium; CH3 slower; CH4 longest.
Patch OUT 1 → VCA, OUT 2 → second VCA, OUT 3 → filter cutoff, OUT 4 → final VCA or reverb send.
Fire a trigger and watch the envelopes cascade one after another.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Four staggered envelopes on OUT 1–4 · unipolar 0–10V (or 0–5V in System Mode).
patchIdeas.hear: A single gate produces a multi-stage swell: fast attack, layered decays, and a long evolving tail as each channel takes over from the last.

patchIdeas.name: Polyrhythmic envelope farm
patchIdeas.patch: CH1 slow CYCLE LFO seeds the rhythm · CH2 EOR-linked fires at each cycle peak · CH3 EOF-linked fires at each cycle bottom.
patchIdeas.diagram:
QUADRAX:
* CH 1: CYCLE (slow)
* CH 2: AD, EOR link
* CH 3: AD, EOF link
- QUADRAX (OUT 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- QUADRAX (OUT 2) >> VCA A (CV)
- QUADRAX (OUT 3) >> VCA B (CV)
steps: Short-press CH1 MODE until green (CYCLE); set RISE and FALL fairly slow for a slow wave.
Short-press CH2 MODE until unlit (AD); short-press CH2 LINK until green (EOR).
Short-press CH3 MODE until unlit (AD); short-press CH3 LINK until yellow (EOF).
Set CH2 and CH3 RISE/FALL short so they make percussive envelopes.
Patch OUT 1 → filter cutoff, OUT 2 → one VCA, OUT 3 → another VCA.
Listen: CH1 slowly sweeps the filter while CH2 and CH3 punch at different phases of that sweep.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 slow unipolar LFO · OUT 2/3 short AD envelopes firing on opposite halves of the cycle.
patchIdeas.hear: A slow breathing filter sweep with two percussive hits per cycle, phased so they feel like a rolling polyrhythm rather than a steady beat.

patchIdeas.name: Internal PWM
patchIdeas.patch: CH1 LFO with SHAPE near square · CH2 slow CYCLE routed via CV A → CH1 FALL (morph), modulating the pulse width dynamically.
patchIdeas.diagram:
QUADRAX:
* CH 1: LFO, Shape=square
* CH 2: CYCLE slow, CV assigned to CH1 Fall
- QUADRAX (OUT 1) -> Mixer (IN) // square with dynamic PWM
- QUADRAX (OUT 2) >> QUADRAX (CV A) // internal morph modulation
steps: Short-press CH1 MODE until magenta (LFO); turn RISE clockwise for audio-rate or medium for sub-LFO.
Adjust SHAPE until the waveform is a square (see LFO shape chart).
Short-press CH2 MODE until green (CYCLE); set CH2 slow.
Patch OUT 2 → CV A input on Quadrax.
Long-press CH1 LINK/CV to enter CV assign mode; press CV A, then press the FALL destination until green (positive attenuversion).
Long-press CH1 LINK/CV to exit CV assign mode.
Patch OUT 1 → mixer or VCA input.
Hear the square pulse width breathe in time with CH2.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — bipolar ±5V square/pulse with morphing width.
patchIdeas.hear: A classic PWM pad or bass tone whose pulse width drifts slowly on its own, no external LFO required.

patchIdeas.name: LFV random CV
patchIdeas.patch: One channel in ALTERNATE LFO (LFV) · RISE sets vacillation rate · FALL sets variance · SHAPE smooths · optional clock into TRIG for tempo-sync.
patchIdeas.diagram:
QUADRAX:
* CH 1: LFO Alternate (LFV)
- Clock (OUT) c> QUADRAX (TRIG 1) // tempo sync
- QUADRAX (OUT 1) >> VCO (FM) // chaotic modulation
steps: Short-press CH1 MODE until magenta (LFO).
Long-press CH1 MODE until it pulses (ALTERNATE / LFV).
Optional: patch a clock into TRIG 1 so vacillations lock to tempo.
Set RISE for rate, FALL for how wildly values jump, SHAPE CW to slew corners smooth.
Patch OUT 1 → a destination like VCO FM, filter cutoff, or wavefolder.
patchIdeas.hear: Organic, wandering modulation that never repeats. Low variance is a drunken walk; high variance is wide random jumps.

patchIdeas.name: Sidechain duck envelope
patchIdeas.patch: CH1 AD on kick trigger for kick VCA · CH2 AD longer on same trigger · CH2 OUT through inverter → bass VCA so bass ducks each kick hit.
patchIdeas.diagram:
QUADRAX:
* CH 1: AD (kick env)
* CH 2: AD long (duck env)
- Kick Trig (Out) t> QUADRAX (TRIG 1)
- Kick Trig (Out) t> QUADRAX (TRIG 2)
- QUADRAX (OUT 1) >> Kick VCA (CV)
- QUADRAX (OUT 2) >> Inverter (In)
- Inverter (Out) >> Bass VCA (CV) // bass ducks on each kick
- Kick VCO (Out) -> Kick VCA (In) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Bass VCO (Out) -> Bass VCA (In) -> Mixer (In 2)
steps: Set CH1 and CH2 to AD mode (MODE unlit).
Multiple the kick trigger into TRIG 1 and TRIG 2.
Set CH1 RISE fast, FALL short — the kick VCA envelope.
Set CH2 RISE fast, FALL longer — the duck envelope.
Patch OUT 1 → kick VCA CV, OUT 2 → inverter → bass VCA CV.
Raise the bass VCA's bias/offset so bass is normally open; each kick envelope now subtracts from it.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — kick envelope (0–10V); OUT 2 — duck envelope (0–10V, inverted downstream).
patchIdeas.hear: Bass dips every time the kick hits, no compressor needed. Adjust CH2 FALL for pump length.

patchIdeas.name: Envelope ↔ LFO mutual modulation
patchIdeas.patch: CH1 CYCLE (self-cycling) and CH2 LFO cross-modulate each other through the CV matrix: OUT 1 → CV A → CH2 rate, OUT 2 → CV B → CH1 rise.
patchIdeas.diagram:
QUADRAX:
* CH 1: CYCLE
* CH 2: LFO
* CV A → CH 2 Rise · CV B → CH 1 Rise
- QUADRAX (OUT 1) >> QUADRAX (CV A) // env modulates LFO rate
- QUADRAX (OUT 2) >> QUADRAX (CV B) // LFO modulates env rate
- QUADRAX (OUT 1) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)
steps: Set CH1 MODE to green (CYCLE) for a self-running envelope.
Set CH2 MODE to magenta (LFO), free-running (TRIG 2 empty).
Patch OUT 1 → CV A, OUT 2 → CV B.
Long-press CH2 LINK/CV; press CV A; press RISE destination to assign CV A → CH2 Rise.
Long-press CH1 LINK/CV; press CV B; press RISE destination to assign CV B → CH1 Rise.
Long-press whichever LINK/CV is flashing magenta to exit CV assign mode.
Patch OUT 1 → a VCA CV input for something audible.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — unipolar cycling envelope used as audible modulator; OUT 2 — bipolar LFO feeding back internally.
patchIdeas.hear: A slowly evolving, never-quite-repeating modulator. Each channel's motion bends the other, giving life to static patches.
